summary: First iteration end-to-end testing of Resilience Brothers P2P platform. 25/25 pytest backend cases passed (public endpoints, auth, role-based access, seed, currency/rate/product CRUD, normal/VIP order math, accumulate→approve balance credit, withdrawals + refund-on-reject, redemptions + stock refund, users admin). Frontend landing, auth redirect, Admin Dashboard + Admin Panel (Resumen/Órdenes/Retiros/Monedas/Tasas/Productos/Usuarios + seed), and full VIP Exchange flow (Radix selects, file upload, success screen) all working with proper VIP rate (393) and 0% commission applied.

minor.issue: Seed is non-idempotent in a useful way (only inserts when collection empty). Calling twice after a partial delete won't re-insert. Acceptable for MVP but worth documenting.

When status transitions from 'approved' -> 'rejected', refund still happens (good). But going 'pending' -> 'approved' -> 'rejected' also refunds (correct). No double-refund guard beyond status check; acceptable.

ui_bugs.issue: Drop-zone visually stays empty after file selection (no filename / preview thumbnail). Upload still works internally.

issues: Role dropdown uses native HTML <select> while the rest of the app uses Radix/shadcn Select — visual inconsistency in dark theme (default OS chrome on select).
Admin user sees 'Cuenta VIP · Tasas preferenciales activas · Sin comisiones' subtitle — semantically correct (backend treats admin as VIP) but could be clarified as 'Cuenta ADMIN'.

action_items: (Optional) Improve proof-upload UX: show selected filename + small thumbnail preview after selection in ExchangeView.
(Optional) Replace native <select> in Admin Users with shadcn Select for visual consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py (~620 lines) is acceptable but approaching the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers (auth/currencies/orders/vip/admin) once new features are added.
/api/orders treats admin as VIP for rate/commission. Confirm this is intended business behaviour (it is currently consistent with VIP balance credit).
Cookie is set with secure=True samesite=None — correct for the preview cross-domain setup.
Mongo _id is consistently excluded via projection {"_id":0} — good.
All Pydantic models use ConfigDict(extra='ignore') — good defensive setup.
data-testid coverage is excellent across Landing, Dashboard, AdminPanel and ExchangeView — testing was unblocked end-to-end.
test_credentials: Seeded directly in MongoDB per /app/auth_testing.md. Tokens: admin=test_session_admin_X, vip=test_session_vip_X, normal=test_session_normal_X. Users: admin.test@resilience.com, vip.test@resilience.com, normal.test@resilience.com.
seed_data_creation: Created 3 test users + 3 sessions in MongoDB (admin/vip/normal). Triggered /api/admin/seed which inserted 6 currencies, 5 rates, 4 products. Test cases also created/deleted TEST_ currencies/rates/products and a couple of VIP orders/withdrawals/redemptions (refunded).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. Test users + sessions are already seeded in MongoDB and reusable. To re-run backend tests: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_p2p_backend.py -v`. Frontend uses Radix shadcn Selects (NOT <select>) for currency/delivery method — interact via clicking trigger then page.get_by_role('option', name='USD'). For VIP exchange success the success screen renders the order id and shows 'Orden creada. Pendiente de verificación.' toast. Note: first real registered user (obryfibowarriors@gmail.com) is now admin in the DB.
rca of the issue: No production bugs found. Backend P2P logic (rate selection, 5%/0% commission, accumulate balance credit, refund flows) is mathematically correct and verified by integration tests against the live preview URL.
